name: Redlynch
overview: Redlynch is a village and large parish on the northern edge of the New Forest National Park in south Wiltshire, a few miles south-east of Salisbury and close to Downton. The parish takes in several settlements including Woodfalls, Lover and Morgan's Vale, and has a primary school, two pubs and a village hall (originally built in 1922 as the church hall at Lover). It offers a peaceful rural lifestyle with the New Forest and Salisbury both within easy reach.
character: A sought-after, spread-out New Forest-fringe village of traditional cottages and family homes, valued for countryside tranquillity with everyday amenities close by.
amenities: Primary school (Morgan's Vale and Woodfalls CofE Primary)
Two pubs: the Kings Head (Redlynch) and the Woodfalls Inn (Woodfalls)
Village shop and nearby farm shops
New Forest National Park on the doorstep

schools.name: Morgan's Vale and Woodfalls CofE Primary School

prices.summary: A desirable New Forest-edge village with everything from cottages to Grade II listed and larger detached homes; values supported by the National Park setting and Downton/Salisbury access. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Walking and cycling in the New Forest National Park
Riding and country pursuits in the surrounding area
Nearby Breamore House and the Avon Valley
pros: On the edge of the New Forest National Park
Retains primary school, pubs and village hall
Quiet rural lifestyle with Salisbury within reach
Good road links to Southampton via the M27
cons: Spread-out parish; amenities are dispersed across settlements
No railway station; rely on Salisbury
Car-dependent for secondary schooling and larger shops
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a tranquil New Forest-fringe village with a primary school and pubs, within reach of Salisbury and the coast.

sources.label: Good Schools Guide - Morgan's Vale and Woodfalls Primary listing
